suspects.name: Morgan Tate
suspects.role: Former yearbook editor; now a local political candidate.
suspects.publicMotive: Blake kept copies of old yearbook and scholarship material that could embarrass several classmates.

questions.text: What was your relationship with Blake?
questions.answer: Blake liked keeping people indebted to him. He called it friendship; everyone else called it leverage.

questions.text: Where were you between 8:34–8:39 p.m.?
questions.answer: I was in the official group photograph when Blake died. Dani's camera should prove that.
questions.autoNote: Morgan Tate's stated alibi: I was in the official group photograph when Blake died. Dani's camera should prove that.

questions.text: What secret did Blake know about you?
questions.answer: Blake kept copies of old yearbook and scholarship material that could embarrass several classmates.

questions.text: Who should we investigate next?
questions.answer: Look at whoever feared the time capsule most. Blake had been pressuring more than one person.

followups.text: The photo was taken at 8:31. Where were you from 8:34 to 8:39?
followups.answer: I stepped away to speak with Blake. I did not think a few minutes mattered until you turned them into an accusation.
followups.autoNote: Morgan Tate admitted the original alibi did not cover the murder window.

suspects.name: Avery Brooks
suspects.role: Former valedictorian; now a teacher and academic adviser.
suspects.publicMotive: I recently learned part of my scholarship paperwork may have survived.

questions.text: What was your relationship with Blake?
questions.answer: Blake knew I never accepted how the scholarship decision was handled. He treated my resentment like entertainment.

questions.text: Where were you between 8:34–8:39 p.m.?
questions.answer: Marcus and I were comparing old newspaper clippings near the dessert display for most of the murder window.
questions.autoNote: Avery Brooks's stated alibi: Marcus and I were comparing old newspaper clippings near the dessert display for most of the murder window.

questions.text: What secret did Blake know about you?
questions.answer: I recently learned part of my scholarship paperwork may have survived.

questions.text: Who should we investigate next?
questions.answer: Anyone Blake was blackmailing had a reason. The difference is who could not survive the public proof.

followups.text: Did Blake ever show you proof that your scholarship application survived?
followups.answer: He showed me only a torn edge and said the complete file would appear tonight. I wanted the truth public, not Blake dead.
followups.autoNote: Avery wanted the scholarship proof revealed, which weakens the motive to silence Blake.

suspects.name: Camille Dupré
suspects.role: Former prom queen; now a business owner and lifestyle personality.
suspects.publicMotive: There were messages between Blake and me that I did not want shown to my family or clients.

questions.text: What was your relationship with Blake?
questions.answer: Blake and I had a complicated history. He saved every message and knew exactly when to use one.

questions.text: Where were you between 8:34–8:39 p.m.?
questions.answer: I told people I was on a private call near the side dining room.
questions.autoNote: Camille Dupré's stated alibi: I told people I was on a private call near the side dining room.

questions.text: What secret did Blake know about you?
questions.answer: There were messages between Blake and me that I did not want shown to my family or clients.

questions.text: Who should we investigate next?
questions.answer: Blake was squeezing people for money and favors. Ask who met him privately tonight.

followups.text: Do the recovered messages prove you met Blake during the murder window?
followups.answer: No. They prove a secret relationship, but the verified call and server record place me away from the crime scene.
followups.autoNote: Camille's messages create a motive but do not break her verified alibi.

suspects.name: Jace Broussard
suspects.role: Former star quarterback; now financially strained.
suspects.publicMotive: I owed Blake more than I could repay this month.

questions.text: What was your relationship with Blake?
questions.answer: Blake loaned me money and made sure I never forgot it.

questions.text: Where were you between 8:34–8:39 p.m.?
questions.answer: Eli and I were outside near the patio doors arguing about the reunion playlist.
questions.autoNote: Jace Broussard's stated alibi: Eli and I were outside near the patio doors arguing about the reunion playlist.

questions.text: What secret did Blake know about you?
questions.answer: I owed Blake more than I could repay this month.

questions.text: Who should we investigate next?
questions.answer: Blake's files mattered more than his money. Someone came to recover a document.

followups.text: Does the loan record place you near Blake at the time of death?
followups.answer: No. It proves debt, not location. Eli and the patio camera can place me away from the photo wall.
followups.autoNote: Jace had a financial motive, but the loan record does not break his patio alibi.

suspects.name: Tessa Nguyen
suspects.role: Former student-council treasurer; now an event planner and reunion organizer.
suspects.publicMotive: The reunion account had discrepancies that looked worse than they were.

questions.text: What was your relationship with Blake?
questions.answer: I organized the reunion. Blake questioned every invoice as if he wanted to catch me doing something wrong.

questions.text: Where were you between 8:34–8:39 p.m.?
questions.answer: I said I was handling a vendor call and checking supplies during the murder window.
questions.autoNote: Tessa Nguyen's stated alibi: I said I was handling a vendor call and checking supplies during the murder window.

questions.text: What secret did Blake know about you?
questions.answer: The reunion account had discrepancies that looked worse than they were.

questions.text: Who should we investigate next?
questions.answer: Blake kept a private list. The wording beside each name may tell you what he planned to reveal.

followups.text: What explains the suspicious reunion account?
followups.answer: The duplicate charge and reimbursement record explain the shortfall. I did not receive hidden money.
followups.autoNote: The ordinary account discrepancy clears Tessa unless another document proves intentional transfers.

suspects.name: Eli Jackson
suspects.role: Former band student; now a local musician.
suspects.publicMotive: I threatened legal action over the song rights.

questions.text: What was your relationship with Blake?
questions.answer: Blake used my song in a property commercial and never paid or credited me.

questions.text: Where were you between 8:34–8:39 p.m.?
questions.answer: Jace and I were near the patio. Several people heard us arguing over the music.
questions.autoNote: Eli Jackson's stated alibi: Jace and I were near the patio. Several people heard us arguing over the music.

questions.text: What secret did Blake know about you?
questions.answer: I threatened legal action over the song rights.

questions.text: Who should we investigate next?
questions.answer: The killer needed a short route to the photo area and a believable reason to leave the room.

followups.text: What does the music-rights document prove?
followups.answer: It proves Blake used my song. It also shows my lawyer scheduled a civil demand, giving me a reason to keep him alive.
followups.autoNote: Eli had a legal path to payment and was witnessed on the patio.

suspects.name: Dani LeBlanc
suspects.role: Former yearbook photographer; now a professional photographer.
suspects.publicMotive: My camera captured more of the hallway and photo backdrop than anyone realized.

questions.text: What was your relationship with Blake?
questions.answer: Blake took credit for one of my photos in high school. I learned to keep originals and timestamps.

questions.text: Where were you between 8:34–8:39 p.m.?
questions.answer: I was photographing the room and checking images on two cameras.
questions.autoNote: Dani LeBlanc's stated alibi: I was photographing the room and checking images on two cameras.

questions.text: What secret did Blake know about you?
questions.answer: My camera captured more of the hallway and photo backdrop than anyone realized.

questions.text: Who should we investigate next?
questions.answer: Compare every alibi to the actual timestamps. Memory is flexible; metadata is not.

followups.text: What did your additional image or record capture?
followups.answer: I captured Morgan entering the photo corridor at 8:35. Her pin was already missing.
followups.autoNote: I captured Morgan entering the photo corridor at 8:35. Her pin was already missing.

suspects.name: Marcus Reed
suspects.role: Former school reporter; now a journalist and podcaster.
suspects.publicMotive: I brought a recorder and planned to publish a reunion episode.

questions.text: What was your relationship with Blake?
questions.answer: Blake killed a scholarship story I wrote in 2006. I came tonight hoping to prove what happened.

questions.text: Where were you between 8:34–8:39 p.m.?
questions.answer: Avery and I were at the newspaper display for most of the murder window.
questions.autoNote: Marcus Reed's stated alibi: Avery and I were at the newspaper display for most of the murder window.

questions.text: What secret did Blake know about you?
questions.answer: I brought a recorder and planned to publish a reunion episode.

questions.text: Who should we investigate next?
questions.answer: Blake's final draft message identifies the person he was actively pressuring.

followups.text: Why did you come prepared to record Blake?
followups.answer: I expected a confession about the old scandal. Publishing the truth required Blake to speak, not die.
followups.autoNote: Marcus's goal depended on Blake making a public statement.

suspects.name: Noah Baptiste
suspects.role: Former class clown; now a radio personality and comedian.
suspects.publicMotive: The threatening note is mine, but I did not kill him.

questions.text: What was your relationship with Blake?
questions.answer: Blake and I never stopped competing for attention. I wrote the warning note as a stupid prank.

questions.text: Where were you between 8:34–8:39 p.m.?
questions.answer: I was testing the microphone near the small stage. The sound system log should show it.
questions.autoNote: Noah Baptiste's stated alibi: I was testing the microphone near the small stage. The sound system log should show it.

questions.text: What secret did Blake know about you?
questions.answer: The threatening note is mine, but I did not kill him.

questions.text: Who should we investigate next?
questions.answer: The note is dramatic, but it is weak evidence. Look for a lie that can be measured.

followups.text: Why should we believe the threat was only a prank?
followups.answer: The note uses paper from the registration table, and my microphone test was logged during the murder window. It was reckless, not lethal.
followups.autoNote: Noah admits the threat, but the sound-system log supports his alibi.

suspects.name: Principal Evelyn Hart
suspects.role: Former assistant principal; now retired.
suspects.publicMotive: I approved decisions in 2006 that I would not defend publicly today.

questions.text: What was your relationship with Blake?
questions.answer: Blake knew the school had buried incidents to protect its reputation.

questions.text: Where were you between 8:34–8:39 p.m.?
questions.answer: I said I remained near Priya and the time-capsule table.
questions.autoNote: Principal Evelyn Hart's stated alibi: I said I remained near Priya and the time-capsule table.

questions.text: What secret did Blake know about you?
questions.answer: I approved decisions in 2006 that I would not defend publicly today.

questions.text: Who should we investigate next?
questions.answer: The original school records could ruin careers and expose the district to a lawsuit.

followups.text: What did Blake know about the old school investigation?
followups.answer: He retained copies of records the administration wanted buried. The records are damaging, but I did not meet him in the corridor tonight.
followups.autoNote: Hart feared the school records, but the case still requires location evidence.

suspects.name: Riley Fontenot
suspects.role: Former outsider; now a technology entrepreneur.
suspects.publicMotive: Blake claimed he deserved ownership because he once introduced me to an investor.

questions.text: What was your relationship with Blake?
questions.answer: Blake bullied me in school and later demanded a piece of my company.

questions.text: Where were you between 8:34–8:39 p.m.?
questions.answer: I was at the bar closing a tab. The receipt and terminal record have times.
questions.autoNote: Riley Fontenot's stated alibi: I was at the bar closing a tab. The receipt and terminal record have times.

questions.text: What secret did Blake know about you?
questions.answer: Blake claimed he deserved ownership because he once introduced me to an investor.

questions.text: Who should we investigate next?
questions.answer: Do not confuse a powerful motive with proof. Find who lied about the five-minute window.

followups.text: What does the ownership demand show?
followups.answer: Blake wanted equity in my company. My bar receipt shows I was completing payment during the critical minutes.
followups.autoNote: Riley had a motive but a timestamped bar transaction supports the alibi.

suspects.name: Dr. Priya Patel
suspects.role: Former science-club president; now a physician and researcher.
suspects.publicMotive: The capsule had been opened before the public ceremony.

questions.text: What was your relationship with Blake?
questions.answer: Blake convinced me to help inspect the time capsule before the reunion.

questions.text: Where were you between 8:34–8:39 p.m.?
questions.answer: I was cataloging capsule items and speaking with several classmates.
questions.autoNote: Dr. Priya Patel's stated alibi: I was cataloging capsule items and speaking with several classmates.

questions.text: What secret did Blake know about you?
questions.answer: The capsule had been opened before the public ceremony.

questions.text: Who should we investigate next?
questions.answer: Someone knew exactly which document or object Blake intended to reveal.

followups.text: Who knew the time capsule had been opened early?
followups.answer: Blake, Principal Hart, Tessa, and I knew. The contents were resealed before guests arrived.
followups.autoNote: Several suspects knew the capsule contents before the ceremony.

evidence.title: Welcome Back, Class of 2006
evidence.teaser: The reunion has become a murder investigation.
facts: The Magnolia Ridge High School Class of 2006 has gathered at Our Mom's Restaurant & Bar.
Former class president Blake Landry promised to expose a secret from senior year.
Minutes later, Blake was found dead near the reunion displays.
evidence.why: The case begins with one question: who needed Blake silenced before his reveal?
evidence.autoNote: Blake planned to reveal a secret from 2006 before he was killed.

evidence.title: Reunion Guest Directory
evidence.teaser: Twelve former classmates and school figures are present.
facts: Every suspect had a relationship with Blake.
Several had motives, but the case must be solved through timelines and physical evidence.
evidence.why: Use the directory to learn who had something to lose.
evidence.autoNote: The reunion includes twelve possible suspects with different secrets and alibis.

evidence.title: Official Reunion Photograph
evidence.teaser: A wide group photo taken shortly before Blake died.
facts: Blake stands near the center of the group.
The reunion displays and several suspect accessories are visible.
Dani's camera stored precise capture metadata.
evidence.why: The photograph establishes what people were wearing and where they were before the murder.
evidence.autoNote: The official reunion photograph may later test a suspect's alibi.

evidence.title: Blake's Final Remarks
evidence.teaser: The final public statement Blake made before his death.
facts: We buried more than memories in that time capsule. We buried a lie that stole someone else's future.
Blake said the proof would be revealed before the night ended.
evidence.why: His wording identifies the category of secret that posed the greatest threat.
evidence.autoNote: Blake announced that documentary proof of an old secret would be revealed that night.

evidence.title: Preliminary Crime Scene Report
evidence.teaser: The murder window and missing display item.
facts: Estimated time of death: 8:34–8:39 p.m..
Blake suffered blunt-force trauma near the photo backdrop. The bronze Most Likely to Succeed trophy was missing, and a campaign pin lay beside the backdrop.
No evidence suggests poison or a weapon brought from outside.
evidence.why: The crime scene narrows the timeline and points toward a nearby display object.
evidence.autoNote: The murder occurred between 8:34–8:39 p.m..

evidence.title: Anonymous Warning Note
evidence.teaser: A handwritten warning found in Blake's belongings.
facts: OPEN THE TIME CAPSULE AND YOU WILL REGRET IT.
The paper came from the reunion registration table.
Noah admits writing a threatening note as a prank.
evidence.why: A threat establishes hostility but does not prove opportunity or method.
evidence.autoNote: Noah wrote a threatening note, but the note alone does not identify the killer.

evidence.title: Draft on Blake's Phone
evidence.teaser: An unsent message naming the person Blake was pressuring.
facts: M.T. has until tonight to cooperate. After that, everyone sees the original file.
The draft was created shortly before Blake's speech.
The referenced file appears to be documentary evidence.
evidence.why: This identifies the active confrontation that was expected to happen that night.
evidence.autoNote: Blake was actively pressuring someone identified by initials before the murder.

evidence.title: Reunion Account Review
evidence.teaser: The finance lead explains whether the reunion shortfall was accidental or intentional.
facts: A duplicate catering charge created the apparent reunion shortfall. Tessa was due a reimbursement, not hiding a theft.
evidence.why: Financial records can create a motive, clear a suspect, or reveal a second layer of deception.
evidence.autoNote: A duplicate catering charge created the apparent reunion shortfall. Tessa was due a reimbursement, not hiding a theft.

evidence.title: Blake's Private Leverage List
evidence.teaser: A list of secrets Blake associated with former classmates.
facts: Camille — messages
Jace — loan
Eli — song
Hart — cover-up
Riley — equity
Morgan — original application
evidence.why: Many people had motives. The most important entry is the one matching Blake's final announcement and phone draft.
evidence.autoNote: Blake maintained leverage over multiple classmates, making motive alone unreliable.

evidence.title: Torn Scholarship Envelope
evidence.teaser: The original document connected to Blake's threatened reveal.
facts: The aged envelope reads 'Aver… Brooks.'
It is labeled Magnolia Parish Leadership Scholarship.
The tear matches a folder found among Blake's papers.
evidence.why: This evidence establishes what the killer stood to lose.
evidence.autoNote: The aged envelope reads 'Aver… Brooks.'

evidence.title: Group Photo Metadata
evidence.teaser: A timestamped record that tests the killer's claimed location.
facts: Morgan said the official group photo covered the murder window.
The image was captured at 8:31 p.m.
Blake died between 8:34 and 8:39 p.m.
evidence.why: A measurable contradiction is stronger than a rumor or an emotional motive.
evidence.autoNote: Blake died between 8:34 and 8:39 p.m.

evidence.title: Blake's Sealed Letter
evidence.teaser: Blake explains the secret he intended to reveal.
facts: Avery Brooks should have received the scholarship. Morgan removed Avery's application and used part of Avery's essay. I kept the original documents because the secret gave me power. Tonight I intended to expose it.
evidence.why: The letter establishes motive, but the physical evidence and false alibi must still identify the killer.
evidence.autoNote: Avery Brooks should have received the scholarship. Morgan removed Avery's application and used part of Avery's essay. I kept the original documents because the secret gave me power. Tonight I intended to expose it.

evidence.title: Campaign Pin Fragment
evidence.teaser: A physical trace links the confrontation to a suspect.
facts: A campaign pin visible on Morgan in the group photo was missing after the murder.
A matching metal clasp was recovered beside the backdrop.
evidence.why: This supports presence at the scene during the relevant interval.
evidence.autoNote: A matching metal clasp was recovered beside the backdrop.

evidence.title: Trophy Recovery Report
evidence.teaser: The missing display object is recovered.
facts: The bronze trophy was found inside a decoration box near Morgan's table.
A cleaned edge retained a trace consistent with the crime scene.
evidence.why: The recovered object connects the crime-scene injury to a specific weapon.
evidence.autoNote: The bronze trophy was found inside a decoration box near Morgan's table.

evidence.title: Dani's Burst-Mode Frame
evidence.teaser: An independent record confirms part of the timeline.
facts: A frame captured at 8:35 p.m. shows Morgan entering the photo corridor.
The campaign pin is no longer visible on her jacket.
evidence.why: Independent corroboration makes the central timeline more reliable.
evidence.autoNote: A frame captured at 8:35 p.m. shows Morgan entering the photo corridor.

evidence.title: Relationship and Message File
evidence.teaser: The relationships lead examines private messages and reputational risks.
facts: Recovered messages show Camille feared embarrassment, but her call log and a server place her in the side dining room.
evidence.why: This may reveal a powerful motive or clear a suspect through verified timing.
evidence.autoNote: Recovered messages show Camille feared embarrassment, but her call log and a server place her in the side dining room.

evidence.title: Debt and Payment File
evidence.teaser: The debt lead examines Blake's loans and repayment pressure.
facts: Jace owed Blake money. A patio camera and Eli's statement place Jace away from the photo area.
evidence.why: Debt creates motive, but a location record may still clear the borrower.
evidence.autoNote: Jace owed Blake money. A patio camera and Eli's statement place Jace away from the photo area.

evidence.title: School Records File
evidence.teaser: The school-records lead examines what administrators and students hid in 2006.
facts: A 2006 memo shows Hart discouraged a scholarship inquiry, but the memo also shows Blake retained the original file himself.
evidence.why: Old institutional records may explain Blake's leverage and who feared the time capsule.
evidence.autoNote: A 2006 memo shows Hart discouraged a scholarship inquiry, but the memo also shows Blake retained the original file himself.

deductions.title: Break the Alibi
deductions.question: Which conclusion is best supported by the murder window and the timestamped record?

options.label: The suspect's stated alibi does not cover the murder window.
The threatening note proves the writer was at the crime scene.
The crime must have happened before the reunion photo.

deductions.success: Correct. The measurable timeline breaks the suspect's stated alibi.
deductions.wrong: That conclusion is not supported by both records. Compare the exact times again.
deductions.autoNote: The decisive timestamp leaves the suspected person unaccounted for during the murder window.

deductions.title: Identify the Motive
deductions.question: What do the original document and Blake's letter establish together?

options.label: Blake planned to expose the secret described in the original document.
The reunion restaurant caused the financial dispute.
No suspect knew what was inside the time capsule.

deductions.success: Correct. The document and letter establish the threatened exposure and motive.
deductions.wrong: Review what Blake said he planned to reveal and who the original document endangered.
deductions.autoNote: Blake's letter confirms the original document was the secret he intended to expose.

deductions.title: Identify the Weapon
deductions.question: Which evidence best connects the recovered object to the murder?

options.label: The physical trace and damage pattern match the recovered display object.
The object belonged to the restaurant, so staff must be involved.
The threatening note names the object directly.

deductions.success: Correct. The trace and damage pattern connect the recovered object to the attack.
deductions.wrong: Focus on the forensic match, not ownership or rumor.
deductions.autoNote: The physical trace and damage pattern identify the missing display object as the weapon.

hints.title: Getting started
levels: Open the case introduction and directory.
The directory helps you understand each possible motive.
After both items are reviewed, the reunion chapter opens automatically.

hints.title: Understanding Blake's threat
levels: Focus on the category of secret Blake planned to reveal.
Compare the wording of his speech with each suspect's public motive.
The speech points toward documentary proof, not a spontaneous argument.

hints.title: Testing the first alibis
levels: Write down the exact murder window.
A threat proves hostility, but a timestamp can prove opportunity.
Blake's draft identifies the person he was actively pressuring that night.

hints.title: Sorting motives from proof
levels: Several leads are red herrings; look for records that explain or verify timing.
The wording beside each name on Blake's list matters.
Complete the lead paths that best match Blake's speech and phone draft.

hints.title: Building the final theory
levels: Use one clue for motive, one for opportunity, and one for the weapon.
Compare E10 and E12 for motive, E05 and E11 for opportunity, and E13 and E14 for method.
The killer is the person whose secret, false alibi, and physical trace all converge.

hints.title: Alibi deduction
levels: Compare the two exact times.
An earlier event cannot cover a later murder window.
The stated alibi leaves the suspect unaccounted for during the critical minutes.

hints.title: Motive deduction
levels: Ask what Blake intended to reveal.
The original document names the threatened secret.
Blake's letter confirms the secret was going public that night.

hints.title: Weapon deduction
levels: Use the physical trace and recovered object together.
The missing display object has a damage pattern matching the crime scene.
The recovered display object is the murder weapon.

solution.motive: Stop Blake from exposing her 2006 scholarship fraud
solution.weapon: Bronze Most Likely to Succeed trophy
solution.clue: Group photo metadata showing the picture was taken at 8:31 p.m.
sequence: Morgan received Blake's demand and met him at the photo backdrop.

Morgan struck him with the bronze trophy.
She hid the trophy in a decoration box and relied on the earlier group photo as an alibi.
solution.confession: Blake had controlled every success I built. I went for the file. When he laughed and promised to destroy me in front of everyone, I reached for the trophy.

evidence.title: Welcome Back, Class of 2006
evidence.teaser: The reunion has become a murder investigation.
facts: The Magnolia Ridge High School Class of 2006 has gathered at Our Mom's Restaurant & Bar.
Former class president Blake Landry promised to expose a secret from senior year.
Minutes later, Blake was found dead near the reunion displays.
evidence.why: The case begins with one question: who needed Blake silenced before his reveal?
evidence.autoNote: Blake planned to reveal a secret from 2006 before he was killed.

evidence.title: Reunion Guest Directory
evidence.teaser: Twelve former classmates and school figures are present.
facts: Every suspect had a relationship with Blake.
Several had motives, but the case must be solved through timelines and physical evidence.
evidence.why: Use the directory to learn who had something to lose.
evidence.autoNote: The reunion includes twelve possible suspects with different secrets and alibis.

evidence.title: Official Reunion Photograph
evidence.teaser: A wide group photo taken shortly before Blake died.
facts: Blake stands near the center of the group.
The reunion displays and several suspect accessories are visible.
Dani's camera stored precise capture metadata.
evidence.why: The photograph establishes what people were wearing and where they were before the murder.
evidence.autoNote: The official reunion photograph may later test a suspect's alibi.

evidence.title: Blake's Final Remarks
evidence.teaser: The final public statement Blake made before his death.
facts: Tonight's time capsule contains an audit trail. Before dessert, everyone will know who turned this reunion into a private bank account.
Blake said the proof would be revealed before the night ended.
evidence.why: His wording identifies the category of secret that posed the greatest threat.
evidence.autoNote: Blake announced that documentary proof of an old secret would be revealed that night.

evidence.title: Preliminary Crime Scene Report
evidence.teaser: The murder window and missing display item.
facts: Estimated time of death: 8:34–8:39 p.m..
Blake suffered blunt-force trauma beside the time-capsule display. The heavy brass lid was missing, and gold paint flakes were found on the carpet.
No evidence suggests poison or a weapon brought from outside.
evidence.why: The crime scene narrows the timeline and points toward a nearby display object.
evidence.autoNote: The murder occurred between 8:34–8:39 p.m..

evidence.title: Anonymous Warning Note
evidence.teaser: A handwritten warning found in Blake's belongings.
facts: STOP DIGGING THROUGH THE REUNION ACCOUNT.
The paper came from the reunion registration table.
Noah admits writing a threatening note as a prank.
evidence.why: A threat establishes hostility but does not prove opportunity or method.
evidence.autoNote: Noah wrote a threatening note, but the note alone does not identify the killer.

evidence.title: Draft on Blake's Phone
evidence.teaser: An unsent message naming the person Blake was pressuring.
facts: T.N. has until tonight to replace every dollar. Then I release the vendor file.
The draft was created shortly before Blake's speech.
The referenced file appears to be documentary evidence.
evidence.why: This identifies the active confrontation that was expected to happen that night.
evidence.autoNote: Blake was actively pressuring someone identified by initials before the murder.

evidence.title: Reunion Account Review

facts: A second spreadsheet reveals the duplicate catering charge was used to disguise separate payments to a fake vendor.
evidence.why: Financial records can create a motive, clear a suspect, or reveal a second layer of deception.
evidence.autoNote: A second spreadsheet reveals the duplicate catering charge was used to disguise separate payments to a fake vendor.

facts: Camille — messages

deductions.title: Break the Alibi
deductions.question: Which conclusion is best supported by the murder window and the timestamped record?

options.label: The suspect's stated alibi does not cover the murder window.
The threatening note proves the writer was at the crime scene.
The crime must have happened before the reunion photo.

deductions.success: Correct. The measurable timeline breaks the suspect's stated alibi.
deductions.wrong: That conclusion is not supported by both records. Compare the exact times again.
deductions.autoNote: The decisive timestamp leaves the suspected person unaccounted for during the murder window.

deductions.title: Identify the Motive
deductions.question: What do the original document and Blake's letter establish together?

options.label: Blake planned to expose the secret described in the original document.
The reunion restaurant caused the financial dispute.
No suspect knew what was inside the time capsule.

deductions.success: Correct. The document and letter establish the threatened exposure and motive.
deductions.wrong: Review what Blake said he planned to reveal and who the original document endangered.
deductions.autoNote: Blake's letter confirms the original document was the secret he intended to expose.

deductions.title: Identify the Weapon
deductions.question: Which evidence best connects the recovered object to the murder?

options.label: The physical trace and damage pattern match the recovered display object.
The object belonged to the restaurant, so staff must be involved.
The threatening note names the object directly.

deductions.success: Correct. The trace and damage pattern connect the recovered object to the attack.
deductions.wrong: Focus on the forensic match, not ownership or rumor.
deductions.autoNote: The physical trace and damage pattern identify the missing display object as the weapon.

evidence.title: Welcome Back, Class of 2006
evidence.teaser: The reunion has become a murder investigation.
facts: The Magnolia Ridge High School Class of 2006 has gathered at Our Mom's Restaurant & Bar.
Former class president Blake Landry promised to expose a secret from senior year.
Minutes later, Blake was found dead near the reunion displays.
evidence.why: The case begins with one question: who needed Blake silenced before his reveal?
evidence.autoNote: Blake planned to reveal a secret from 2006 before he was killed.

evidence.title: Reunion Guest Directory
evidence.teaser: Twelve former classmates and school figures are present.
facts: Every suspect had a relationship with Blake.

evidence.autoNote: The reunion includes twelve possible suspects with different secrets and alibis.

evidence.title: Official Reunion Photograph
evidence.teaser: A wide group photo taken shortly before Blake died.

evidence.title: Blake's Final Remarks
evidence.teaser: The final public statement Blake made before his death.
facts: Some reunion memories were never meant for social media. Tonight I am showing the messages one classmate paid me to keep hidden.

evidence.why: His wording identifies the category of secret that posed the greatest threat.

evidence.title: Preliminary Crime Scene Report

facts: Estimated time of death: 8:34–8:39 p.m..
Blake suffered blunt-force trauma near the senior-awards display. A heavy glass plaque was missing, and a jeweled earring back was found near the body.
No evidence suggests poison or a weapon brought from outside.

evidence.autoNote: The murder occurred between 8:34–8:39 p.m..

evidence.title: Anonymous Warning Note

facts: DELETE THE MESSAGES OR YOU WILL LOSE EVERYTHING.

deductions.title: Break the Alibi
deductions.question: Which conclusion is best supported by the murder window and the timestamped record?

options.label: The suspect's stated alibi does not cover the murder window.
The threatening note proves the writer was at the crime scene.
The crime must have happened before the reunion photo.

deductions.success: Correct. The measurable timeline breaks the suspect's stated alibi.
deductions.wrong: That conclusion is not supported by both records. Compare the exact times again.
deductions.autoNote: The decisive timestamp leaves the suspected person unaccounted for during the murder window.

deductions.title: Identify the Motive
deductions.question: What do the original document and Blake's letter establish together?

options.label: Blake planned to expose the secret described in the original document.
The reunion restaurant caused the financial dispute.
No suspect knew what was inside the time capsule.

deductions.success: Correct. The document and letter establish the threatened exposure and motive.
deductions.wrong: Review what Blake said he planned to reveal and who the original document endangered.
deductions.autoNote: Blake's letter confirms the original document was the secret he intended to expose.

deductions.title: Identify the Weapon
deductions.question: Which evidence best connects the recovered object to the murder?

options.label: The physical trace and damage pattern match the recovered display object.
The object belonged to the restaurant, so staff must be involved.
The threatening note names the object directly.

deductions.success: Correct. The trace and damage pattern connect the recovered object to the attack.
deductions.wrong: Focus on the forensic match, not ownership or rumor.
deductions.autoNote: The physical trace and damage pattern identify the missing display object as the weapon.

followups.answer: Blake, Principal Hart, Tessa, and I knew. The contents were resealed before guests arrived.
followups.autoNote: Several suspects knew the capsule contents before the ceremony.

evidence.title: Welcome Back, Class of 2006
evidence.teaser: The reunion has become a murder investigation.
facts: The Magnolia Ridge High School Class of 2006 has gathered at Our Mom's Restaurant & Bar.
Former class president Blake Landry promised to expose a secret from senior year.
Minutes later, Blake was found dead near the reunion displays.
evidence.why: The case begins with one question: who needed Blake silenced before his reveal?
evidence.autoNote: Blake planned to reveal a secret from 2006 before he was killed.

evidence.title: Reunion Guest Directory
evidence.teaser: Twelve former classmates and school figures are present.
facts: Every suspect had a relationship with Blake.
Several had motives, but the case must be solved through timelines and physical evidence.
evidence.why: Use the directory to learn who had something to lose.
evidence.autoNote: The reunion includes twelve possible suspects with different secrets and alibis.

evidence.title: Official Reunion Photograph
evidence.teaser: A wide group photo taken shortly before Blake died.
facts: Blake stands near the center of the group.
The reunion displays and several suspect accessories are visible.
Dani's camera stored precise capture metadata.
evidence.why: The photograph establishes what people were wearing and where they were before the murder.
evidence.autoNote: The official reunion photograph may later test a suspect's alibi.

evidence.title: Blake's Final Remarks
evidence.teaser: The final public statement Blake made before his death.
facts: The time capsule holds the report the school said never existed. Tonight the Class of 2006 learns who was protected and who was sacrificed.
Blake said the proof would be revealed before the night ended.
evidence.why: His wording identifies the category of secret that posed the greatest threat.
evidence.autoNote: Blake announced that documentary proof of an old secret would be revealed that night.

evidence.title: Preliminary Crime Scene Report
evidence.teaser: The murder window and missing display item.
facts: Estimated time of death: 8:34–8:39 p.m..
Blake suffered blunt-force trauma near the yearbook display. The framed Class of 2006 cover was missing, and a sliver of frame glass was found on his jacket.
No evidence suggests poison or a weapon brought from outside.
evidence.why: The crime scene narrows the timeline and points toward a nearby display object.
evidence.autoNote: The murder occurred between 8:34–8:39 p.m..

evidence.title: Anonymous Warning Note
evidence.teaser: A handwritten warning found in Blake's belongings.
facts: SOME RECORDS SHOULD STAY BURIED.
The paper came from the reunion registration table.
Noah admits writing a threatening note as a prank.
evidence.why: A threat establishes hostility but does not prove opportunity or method.
evidence.autoNote: Noah wrote a threatening note, but the note alone does not identify the killer.

evidence.title: Draft on Blake's Phone
evidence.teaser: An unsent message naming the person Blake was pressuring.
facts: E.H. has until tonight to admit who altered the injury report. Then I release the original.
The draft was created shortly before Blake's speech.
The referenced file appears to be documentary evidence.
evidence.why: This identifies the active confrontation that was expected to happen that night.
evidence.autoNote: Blake was actively pressuring someone identified by initials before the murder.

evidence.title: Reunion Account Review
evidence.teaser: The finance lead explains whether the reunion shortfall was accidental or intentional.
facts: The reunion money discrepancy was a duplicate charge and was corrected before dinner.
evidence.why: Financial records can create a motive, clear a suspect, or reveal a second layer of deception.
evidence.autoNote: The reunion money discrepancy was a duplicate charge and was corrected before dinner.

evidence.title: Blake's Private Leverage List
evidence.teaser: A list of secrets Blake associated with former classmates.
facts: Morgan — scholarship
Camille — messages
Jace — loan
Tessa — accounts
Riley — injury claim
Hart — original report
evidence.why: Many people had motives. The most important entry is the one matching Blake's final announcement and phone draft.
evidence.autoNote: Blake maintained leverage over multiple classmates, making motive alone unreliable.

evidence.title: Original 2006 Hazing Report
evidence.teaser: The original document connected to Blake's threatened reveal.
facts: The report describes Riley's injury during an unsanctioned senior initiation.
A later official version removes the names of two protected students.
Hart signed the instruction to replace the original report.
evidence.why: This evidence establishes what the killer stood to lose.
evidence.autoNote: The report describes Riley's injury during an unsanctioned senior initiation.

evidence.title: Display Corridor Access Record
evidence.teaser: A timestamped record that tests the killer's claimed location.
facts: Hart said she never left the time-capsule table.
The badge assigned to Hart opened the display corridor at 8:35 p.m.
It returned to the dining room at 8:40 p.m.
evidence.why: A measurable contradiction is stronger than a rumor or an emotional motive.
evidence.autoNote: It returned to the dining room at 8:40 p.m.

evidence.title: Blake's Sealed Letter
evidence.teaser: Blake explains the secret he intended to reveal.
facts: Principal Hart ordered the original hazing report replaced to protect the school and influential families. Riley carried the consequences. I kept the signed original and planned to reveal it tonight.
evidence.why: The letter establishes motive, but the physical evidence and false alibi must still identify the killer.
evidence.autoNote: Principal Hart ordered the original hazing report replaced to protect the school and influential families. Riley carried the consequences. I kept the signed original and planned to reveal it tonight.

evidence.title: Frame Glass in Hart's Coat
evidence.teaser: A physical trace links the confrontation to a suspect.
facts: A fine glass sliver was recovered from the cuff of Hart's coat.
Its tint and thickness match the missing framed yearbook display.
evidence.why: This supports presence at the scene during the relevant interval.
evidence.autoNote: Its tint and thickness match the missing framed yearbook display.

evidence.title: Framed Yearbook Recovery
evidence.teaser: The missing display object is recovered.
facts: The frame was found behind folded reunion banners.
A damaged corner matches the impact pattern and the glass on Blake's jacket.
evidence.why: The recovered object connects the crime-scene injury to a specific weapon.
evidence.autoNote: The frame was found behind folded reunion banners.

evidence.title: Priya's Inventory Statement
evidence.teaser: An independent record confirms part of the timeline.
facts: Priya says Hart left the capsule table at approximately 8:33 p.m.
Hart returned after the crash and claimed she had only stepped away for water.
evidence.why: Independent corroboration makes the central timeline more reliable.
evidence.autoNote: Priya says Hart left the capsule table at approximately 8:33 p.m.

evidence.title: Relationship and Message File
evidence.teaser: The relationships lead examines private messages and reputational risks.
facts: Camille's messages created a motive, but her call and server receipt cover the critical minutes.
evidence.why: This may reveal a powerful motive or clear a suspect through verified timing.
evidence.autoNote: Camille's messages created a motive, but her call and server receipt cover the critical minutes.

evidence.title: Debt and Payment File
evidence.teaser: The debt lead examines Blake's loans and repayment pressure.
facts: Jace's debt was serious, but he remained on the patio with Eli.
evidence.why: Debt creates motive, but a location record may still clear the borrower.
evidence.autoNote: Jace's debt was serious, but he remained on the patio with Eli.

evidence.title: School Records File
evidence.teaser: The school-records lead examines what administrators and students hid in 2006.
facts: The original hazing report and Hart's signature establish a direct threat to Hart's reputation, pension, and the district.
evidence.why: Old institutional records may explain Blake's leverage and who feared the time capsule.
evidence.autoNote: The original hazing report and Hart's signature establish a direct threat to Hart's reputation, pension, and the district.

leads.title: Follow the Money
leads.description: Review reunion invoices, reimbursements, and unexplained payments.

leads.title: Private Relationships
leads.description: Examine messages, affairs, and reputational blackmail.

leads.title: Loans and Debts
leads.description: Check who owed Blake money and whether the debt explains the murder.

leads.title: Buried School Records
leads.description: Investigate the 2006 files Blake threatened to release.

deductions.title: Break the Alibi
deductions.question: Which conclusion is best supported by the murder window and the timestamped record?

options.label: The suspect's stated alibi does not cover the murder window.
The threatening note proves the writer was at the crime scene.
The crime must have happened before the reunion photo.

deductions.success: Correct. The measurable timeline breaks the suspect's stated alibi.
deductions.wrong: That conclusion is not supported by both records. Compare the exact times again.
deductions.autoNote: The decisive timestamp leaves the suspected person unaccounted for during the murder window.

deductions.title: Identify the Motive
deductions.question: What do the original document and Blake's letter establish together?

options.label: Blake planned to expose the secret described in the original document.
The reunion restaurant caused the financial dispute.
No suspect knew what was inside the time capsule.

deductions.success: Correct. The document and letter establish the threatened exposure and motive.
deductions.wrong: Review what Blake said he planned to reveal and who the original document endangered.
deductions.autoNote: Blake's letter confirms the original document was the secret he intended to expose.

deductions.title: Identify the Weapon
deductions.question: Which evidence best connects the recovered object to the murder?

options.label: The physical trace and damage pattern match the recovered display object.
The object belonged to the restaurant, so staff must be involved.
The threatening note names the object directly.

deductions.success: Correct. The trace and damage pattern connect the recovered object to the attack.
deductions.wrong: Focus on the forensic match, not ownership or rumor.
deductions.autoNote: The physical trace and damage pattern identify the missing display object as the weapon.

hints.title: Getting started
levels: Open the case introduction and directory.
The directory helps you understand each possible motive.
After both items are reviewed, the reunion chapter opens automatically.

hints.title: Understanding Blake's threat
levels: Focus on the category of secret Blake planned to reveal.
Compare the wording of his speech with each suspect's public motive.
The speech points toward documentary proof, not a spontaneous argument.

hints.title: Testing the first alibis
levels: Write down the exact murder window.
A threat proves hostility, but a timestamp can prove opportunity.
Blake's draft identifies the person he was actively pressuring that night.

hints.title: Sorting motives from proof
levels: Several leads are red herrings; look for records that explain or verify timing.
The wording beside each name on Blake's list matters.
Complete the lead paths that best match Blake's speech and phone draft.

hints.title: Building the final theory
levels: Use one clue for motive, one for opportunity, and one for the weapon.
Compare E10 and E12 for motive, E05 and E11 for opportunity, and E13 and E14 for method.
The killer is the person whose secret, false alibi, and physical trace all converge.

hints.title: Alibi deduction
levels: Compare the two exact times.
An earlier event cannot cover a later murder window.
The stated alibi leaves the suspect unaccounted for during the critical minutes.

hints.title: Motive deduction
levels: Ask what Blake intended to reveal.
The original document names the threatened secret.
Blake's letter confirms the secret was going public that night.

hints.title: Weapon deduction
levels: Use the physical trace and recovered object together.
The missing display object has a damage pattern matching the crime scene.
The recovered display object is the murder weapon.

motives.label: Prevent exposure of a concealed 2006 hazing injury and falsified school report
Prevent an affair from becoming public

clues.label: A location-access record showing Hart's assigned badge entered the display corridor during the murder window

solution.motive: Prevent exposure of a concealed 2006 hazing injury and falsified school report
solution.clue: A location-access record showing Hart's assigned badge entered the display corridor during the murder window
sequence: Hart learned Blake had the signed original hazing report.
She followed him into the display corridor and demanded it.
She struck him with the framed yearbook display.
She hid the frame behind banners and denied leaving the time-capsule table.
solution.confession: I spent twenty years telling myself I protected the school. Blake was going to turn one decision into my entire life. I followed him for the report, and the argument became something I could not undo.
